summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Two-way table'?

"Two-way table" means: A table displaying observed counts for every combination of two categorical variables, with row totals, column totals, and grand total. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Association in two-way tables'?

"Association in two-way tables" means: Two variables are associated if conditional distributions of one variable differ across categories of the other. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Segmented bar chart'?

"Segmented bar chart" means: A graphical display of conditional distributions; bars divided into segments showing the proportion in each category of the response variable. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Marginal distribution'?

In Two-Way Tables and Conditional Distributions, "Marginal distribution" refers to: The distribution of one variable ignoring the other; calculated from the row or column totals divided by the grand total.

What is the definition of 'Conditional distribution'?

In Two-Way Tables and Conditional Distributions, "Conditional distribution" refers to: The distribution of one variable within a specific category of the other; cell counts divided by the appropriate row or column total.
